name: Pepper and Bread Salad
recipeIngredient: 1 Green onion, white and green parts, minced
recipeInstructions:
1. Spear the pepper with a fork and hold over an open flame until it blisters all over. Place in a plastic bag and set aside until the skin loosens, about 30 minutes.
2. Meanwhile, place the bread cubes in a colander and hold under cold water to dampen. Squeeze out the excess water and transfer to a salad bowl.
3. Add the capers, thyme, green onion, and celery to the bowl with the bread cubes.
4. Using a sharp paring knife, carefully peel the skin off the pepper. Core and dice into half-inch pieces.
5. Add the diced pepper to the salad.
6. In a small cup, stir together the olive oil, sherry vinegar, prepared Dijon mustard, and freshly ground pepper until well combined.
7. Pour the dressing over the salad and toss gently until everything is evenly coated.
NOTE: A high-quality crusty French bread is essential for this salad. It should be at least one day old. If it is still soft, place the bread cubes on a baking sheet and toast in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for 5 to 10 minutes.
